{{Quote|Kadir, can you really be so innocent? Politics is a game.|Grand Moff Trachta|<ref name="Star Wars: Empire: Betrayal">''[[Star Wars: Empire: Betrayal]]''</ref>}}
'''Kadir''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] who served as [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]]. He became one of the conspirators in [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Trachta]]'s [[Anti-Sith conspiracy|plot]] to overthrow the [[Order of the Sith Lords]] and take control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Kadir was accompanied by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Trachta]] in [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]]. Kadir and Trachta witnessed as Vader tested the [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] accuracy of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Impressed by their skill with the [[E-11 blaster rifle/Legends|E-11 blaster rifles]], Vader ordered an entire stormtrooper [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] to escort Grand Moff [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. However, Kadir rejected the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] orders, because the stormtroopers were supposed to be assigned to [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]]. Vader reminded him that [[Kadir's father]] and predecessor in the CSF was executed for demanding more soldiers than he was given. Vader recommended Kadir not to express his views unless he wanted to share the fate of his predecessor. Kadir was angered by Vader's arrogant attitude, but he had to, albeit reluctantly, transfer the stormtroopers to Tarkin's ship.

The incident sparked the beginning of Kadir's hostile stance on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and he contemned the fact that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and Darth Vader were the most powerful men of the Empire. He and Trachta discussed the subject, and Kadir explained his distrust of the secretive nature of the Sith. Sensing Kadir's hatred of the Sith, Trachta soon recruited Kadir to his [[Anti-Sith conspiracy|conspiracy]] to eliminate both Emperor Palpatine and Darth Vader. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Skosef]] introduced Kadir during the meeting of the conspirators, but the [[Near-Human]] assassin [[Gauer]], one of Trachta's associates, accused Skosef of exposing the conspiracy to an outsider. However, Trachta silenced Gauer, explaining that Kadir was the newest member in the conspiracy.

[[File:BetrayalCoup.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Kadir and the [[Clone stormtrooper|stormtroopers]] in the [[Imperial Palace throne room (Anti-Sith conspiracy)|Emperor's throne room]].]]
For over a decade, Trachta had secretly masterminded a sophisticated plot to destroy the Sith and seize control of the Empire. The plan involved [[Clone stormtrooper|cloned stormtroopers]] that were "programmed" and trained loyal, not to the Emperor, but to Trachta and his affiliates. However, the rivalry between Trachta and his fellow Grand Moff [[Bartam]], one of the key members in the conspiracy, compromised the stability of the plot. Kadir was personally requested by Trachta to observe the corpulent Grand Moff's loyalty to the conspirators. Both Trachta and Bartam were determined to assassinate each other, and Kadir remained on the background during the discord between the scheming Grand Moffs. As a result, Trachta was assassinated by Gauer, while Skosef executed Bartam, only to be killed by a stormtrooper guard. Without Trachta and Bartam, as well as Skosef, the only living members of the conspiracy were Kadir, Gauer, and [[Second officer/Legends|Second Officer]] [[Dezsetes]] aboard the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Mathayus]]''. Kadir became the leader of the remaining conspirators, determined to accomplish the final stage of the plot by eliminating the Emperor and taking control of the Imperial state.

Kadir and Gauer, supported by Trachta's clone stormtroopers, marched to the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]]. Inside the building, two officers blocked the corridor, as the stormtroopers were not allowed enter the [[Imperial Palace throne room (Anti-Sith conspiracy)|Emperor's throne room]], but both of them were killed by a clone stormtrooper and Gauer. Kadir and the stormtroopers stepped in the Emperor's throne room, preparing to kill Palpatine. However, the assassins were ambushed by the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsmen]], slaughtering numerous stormtroopers. Meanwhile, Gauer abandoned his ally and fled from the Palace. The remaining stormtroopers were wiped out by the Emperor's powerful [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. However, Kadir was knocked out by one of the Royal Guards earlier during the assassination attempt, which saved him from being electrocuted by the lightning bolts. When Kadir regained his consciousness, the stormtroopers were dead and the Moff took his last chance to take out the Emperor. Desperate, he hoped to kill Palpatine with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], but the blaster bolt harmlessly missed the [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]]. The Emperor hurled Kadir across the room with Force lightning while praising him for exterminating the enemies of the Empire.

[[File:KadirPalpatine1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] punishes Kadir for his treachery.]]
Palpatine was going to kill the treacherous Moff, but Kadir tried to save his life by convincing him that the conspirators had eliminated Vader. He offered himself as Palpatine's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], but the Emperor declined the offer, knowing that Kadir lacked [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]]. The Emperor activated a [[Holocamera/Legends|holocam]] on the throne, showing Vader confronting Dezsetes and the clone stormtroopers aboard the ''Mathayus''. Kadir witnessed as Vader killed the stormtroopers and interrogated Dezsetes, who confessed that Trachta was the mastermind behind the conspiracy. Dezsetes was then killed by Vader, and in the Emperor's throne room Palpatine swiftly electrocuted the unlucky Moff with a deadly torrent of Force lightning. The deaths of Kadir and Dezsetes ended the anti-Sith conspiracy.

==Behind the scenes==
Kadir is sometimes assumed to be Moff of [[Coruscant sector/Legends|Imperial sector]], including the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and a number of other star systems. In fact, there is no evidence that he is anything more than the head of [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]], which would, by implication, be constituted as a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in its own right, just as Imperial Sector is part of [[Imperial Center Oversector]].
